mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding employment, boosting consumer income and spending, which drives corporate earnings and equity prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS increases fail to beat median market return (sign test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A high and persistent unemployment rate signals labor market slack, weak aggregate demand, and elevated recession risk, depressing equity valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 8%, the 60-day forward return distribution is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05 vs zero) or the hypothesis fails to predict negative returns in at least 55% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in retail sales combined with a very low federal funds rate signals robust consumer demand unconstrained by monetary drag, creating a powerful tailwind for corporate profits and equity prices.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with strong consumption
failure_condition: The joint signal (RSAFS positive and FEDFUNDS low) fails to produce median 20-day forward returns significantly above the unconditional median (Wilcoxon p > 0.05) or hit rate < 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising consumer prices force the Fed to raise rates further, increasing the discount rate on future equity cash flows and compressing valuations, leading to negative equity returns over the quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.5% and FEDFUNDS increasing over 3 months
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 0.5% and rising, CPIAUCSL increases are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of cases, or the negative return proportion is not statistically significant (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong household demand and construction activity, which boosts GDP growth, employment, and consumer confidence, driving equity prices higher over the following month.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return after HOUST increases is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the rank IC between HOUST changes and returns is below 0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls to an extremely low level, it signals aggressive monetary accommodation, reducing borrowing costs and encouraging risk-taking, causing an immediate short-term rally in equities.
failure_condition: Following FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the average 5-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the proportion of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside shrinking retail sales signals an inventory build-up relative to demand, indicating a future slowdown in output and earnings, which depresses equity prices over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change > 0.5% and RSAFS MoM change < -0.5%
failure_condition: When the divergence signal is active, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of cases, or the signal does not yield a significantly negative mean return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime with very high unemployment, an improvement in retail sales signals the early stages of recovery, boosting investor confidence and leading to positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7%, RSAFS increases are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
